title: A-SDLC Step Definition
description: Schema for individual step files at stages/NN-name/steps/step-N-N.yaml. Steps are the atomic units of work within a stage — they trigger a control (optional), consume input artifacts, and produce output artifacts. Steps are executed by roles.

id.description: Step identifier matching the step number (e.g., '1.1', '1.5b', '3.2-sc3c', '4.7-ai-adversarial'). Must match the filename stem after 'step-' with dots replaced by hyphens.

stage.description: Stage number this step belongs to.

control.description: Control ID triggered by this step. Null for procedural/handover steps.

description.description: Brief description of what happens in this step.

delegation.description: Human-readable delegation pattern describing who does what (e.g., 'Fully automated', 'Agent drafts → human approves').

depends_on.description: Step IDs this step depends on. Empty array means this step has no prerequisites within the stage.

condition.description: Conditionality text. Null or absent if the step is unconditional.

frequency.description: Execution frequency. Defaults to per_change. Stage 6 steps often use 'continuous'.

actors.description: Ordered list of role-action pairs defining who does what in this step.

role.description: Role code (AGT, DEV, QA, SA, PO, RO, AGL, etc.).

action.description: Action this actor takes in this step.

inputs.description: Input descriptions or artifact references consumed by this step.

outputs.description: Output descriptions or artifact references produced by this step.

on_failure.description: What happens if this step fails or is blocked.

on_uncertainty.description: What happens if the step result is uncertain or ambiguous.

on_changes_requested.description: What happens if changes are requested during a review step.

note.description: Additional note or clarification for this step.

tables.description: Supplementary reference tables (e.g., approval authority by risk tier).
